description: Quantify the structural polarity of a cell by measuring the displacement between its cell center and nuclear center using the first available single-cell image pair in ~/Fiji_Data/raw/BBBC005/ (filenames containing 'C1' and matching 'w1' for Cell Body and 'w2' for Nucleus). Set the spatial scale for the images to 0.65 µm/pixel. Create a composite image with the Cell Body in green and the Nucleus in red, then measure the straight-line distance between their centroids in microns. Annotate the composite with a white arrow or line connecting the cell center to the nuclear center and flatten the overlay. Save the annotated image to ~/Fiji_Data/results/polarity/annotated_cell.png and the measurements to ~/Fiji_Data/results/polarity/displacement_metrics.csv with columns: Cell_X, Cell_Y, Nucleus_X, Nucleus_Y, Displacement_um.
